title: SPARTA Strategy Factory v1 — Research-Only Automation Backbone

relationship_to_existing_work.principle: Strategy Factory v1 BUILDS ON the read-only routine layer; it does not replace or duplicate it. Reuse the candidate registry, the routine-layer queue, the frozen Crypto-D1 dataset, and tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py.
relationship_to_existing_work.adds: queue -> orchestrator -> runner-adapter -> report -> decision-memo -> registry -> dashboard-feed backbone with a machine-enforced safety-gate contract

section_1_current_state.best_candidate_evidence: OOS positive and floor-backed on all 3 assets (BTC 32 / ETH 31 / SOL 23 trades vs floor 20); N=30 floor-backed on BTC only (ETH 19 / SOL 18 sample-thin)
section_1_current_state.jarvis_lane_monitor: read-only, dynamic, committed at 3bd2633; hard-clamps the four authorization flags to false
section_1_current_state.paper_live_authorization: none anywhere; no broker/exchange/order/fetch path exists

section_2_purpose.is: a deterministic, research-only automation factory that runs already-approved experiments through an allow-listed offline runner against frozen data and emits standard reports, decision memos, and a machine-readable registry
section_2_purpose.single_goal: remove manual copy-paste from the research loop; change who types the commands, not what is allowed to happen
section_2_purpose.safety_preservation: every existing property (offline, frozen data, WATCH ceiling, no promotion) is preserved and made machine-enforced rather than convention-enforced

section_3_components.name: Strategy Report Registry v1
section_3_components.role: single source of truth; JSON+MD listing every strategy with latest verdict/run_id/report_path/decision_memo_path/safety_flags/next_action; built by read-only scan, updated additively

section_3_components.name: Research Queue v1
section_3_components.role: human-authored version-controlled JSON backlog; nothing runs unless queued AND approved_for_research with zero blocked_reasons

section_3_components.name: Research-only Orchestrator v1
section_3_components.role: reads queue, validates safety contract, invokes allow-listed runner; DEFAULT mode is dry-run (validate+plan+manifest, run nothing)

section_3_components.name: Runner Adapter layer
section_3_components.role: explicit allow-list mapping allowed_runner -> known offline runner (initially only crypto_d1_backtest_runner); rejects unknown runners; never builds broker/exchange/order/fetch calls

section_3_components.name: Auto Decision Memo v1
section_3_components.role: after an executed run, generate a DRAFT memo (verdict echo, sample-sufficiency nuance, recommended next checkpoint) marked requires_operator_signoff=true; records, never promotes

section_3_components.name: Dashboard Registry Feed
section_3_components.role: read-only adapter exposing the registry to JARVIS; GET-only, hard-false auth flags, forbidden-verdict clamp, fail-closed

section_3_components.name: Safety Gate Contract
section_3_components.role: single declarative config configs/strategy_factory_safety.json; every component loads and enforces it; orchestrator refuses to act if missing/malformed/weakened

section_3_components.name: Failure Classifier
section_3_components.role: deterministic closed vocabulary: QUEUE_REJECTED, SAFETY_BLOCKED, RUNNER_NOT_ALLOWED, RUN_ERROR, REPORT_MISSING, OK_WATCH, OK_FAIL; never emits PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG

section_3_components.name: Audit / Run Manifest
section_3_components.role: every orchestrator invocation writes a manifest (inputs, resolved safety flags, runner, dataset hash, start/stop, outcome, classifier label) so any run is reconstructable from disk

section_4_file_architecture.note: FUTURE files. NONE are created by this plan except the two plan files below.
constraints: configs/ does not exist yet; created by a future step
all new tools are Python standard library only
nothing here is created by the present plan except the two plan files

section_5_data_flow: -> tools/strategy_factory.py loads configs/strategy_factory_safety.json; validate each item; fail closed on violation
-> Runner Adapter (allow-list) -> tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py (offline, frozen data); execute mode only; dry-run default writes plan+manifest and runs nothing
-> Dashboard Registry Feed -> JARVIS /api/jarvis/status (read-only, GET, clamped)
data_flow_invariants: every arrow is one-directional and read-or-append-only downstream of the queue; the orchestrator never writes upstream of itself and never mutates frozen datasets

enforcement_rules: orchestrator loads the contract first; missing/malformed/weakened contract => hard stop, run nothing
any queue item requesting a disabled capability is rejected and recorded with a blocked_reason; never silently downgraded into running
dangerous actions require explicit human approval and are out of scope for the automated path in v1
verdict ceiling is WATCH; any PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG seen in a result is clamped

scope: Crypto-D1 Momentum N=20 deeper validation using the existing frozen dataset and existing offline runner
read-only registry scan of existing Crypto-D1 reports (run nothing)
no new live data, no new dataset freeze, no fetch

section_8_registry_design.construction: read-only scan first (derive from committed reports), then additive update after orchestrated runs
section_8_registry_design.verdict_rule: never invents a verdict; echoes the clamped runner output (WATCH ceiling; never PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG)

section_9_queue_design.run_condition: runs only if approved_for_research is true, blocked_reasons is empty, and allowed_runner/allowed_dataset pass the contract allow-list; otherwise skipped with a recorded reason

section_10_orchestrator_behavior: load + validate the Safety Gate Contract; hard-stop if missing/weakened
validate each item against the contract and the allow-lists
run only approved research tasks, only via the Runner Adapter allow-list, against frozen data
fail closed: skip-and-classify on any validation failure, missing input, or runner error; no improvised fallback that does more than approved
write an audit manifest for every invocation (whether or not anything ran)
never trade, never place/route an order, never touch a broker/exchange
never mutate frozen data (verify dataset checksums; refuse on mismatch)
stop on unsafe config rather than proceeding with a degraded contract
produce a clear operator report (eligible / ran / blocked-and-why / verdicts / next actions)
DEFAULT mode is dry-run (validate+plan+manifest, execute nothing); execute mode is a separate explicitly-approved invocation

section_11_dashboard_integration.end_state: single registry-driven JARVIS feed instead of bespoke per-lane scanners
section_11_dashboard_integration.mechanism: read-only _jarvis_strategy_registry_feed() (same pattern as the shipped Lane Monitor: GET-only, hard-false auth flags, forbidden-verdict clamp, fail-closed) renders one row per strategy from registry.json
section_11_dashboard_integration.migration: additive and staged; registry feed ships alongside the existing Lane Monitor and the Lane Monitor is only retired once the feed reproduces its content; NO dashboard change in this plan

section_12_tests_required: queue validation: schema/required fields; approved/blocked gating; allow-list enforcement; malformed queue fails closed
safety flags: every flag pinned false as specified; missing/weakened contract => hard stop; human_approval_required cannot be toggled off via a queue item
runner allow-list: only allow-listed runners resolve; unknown runner => RUNNER_NOT_ALLOWED, nothing executed
no broker/order paths: assert no broker/exchange/order/fetch/paper/live symbol reachable; monkeypatch subprocess/network to raise and prove never called in dry-run
registry generation: read-only scan produces correct records from seeded reports; verdict clamped to WATCH; additive update does not drop existing records
fail-closed behavior: missing input, checksum mismatch, contract error all skip-and-record, never proceed
dashboard feed read-only (future): hard-false auth flags, clamps PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG, no execution affordance

section_13_roadmap.name: Queue schema
section_13_roadmap.deliverable: configs/research_queue.json schema + loader + test_strategy_factory_queue.py (no orchestration yet)

section_13_roadmap.name: Dashboard registry feed
section_13_roadmap.deliverable: read-only JARVIS feed over the registry, additive alongside the Lane Monitor

section_13_roadmap.name: Nightly scheduler (later, separately approved)
section_13_roadmap.deliverable: opt-in local scheduler, still research-only, still dry-run by default; only after Steps 1-6 proven

roadmap_rule: each step is its own commit, gated on its tests, separately approved before it runs; execute mode for the orchestrator is a distinct approval after dry-run is trusted
section_14_non_authorization_statement: This document is a PLAN ONLY. It writes no tool, no config, no test, no dashboard change, no dataset change, and runs no backtest. It authorizes no paper or live trading, no broker/exchange/order/fetch path, no ACTIVE/STRONG promotion, and no Bundle 23. Crypto-D1 remains WATCH / MIXED and NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA. The current best candidate (Crypto-D1 Momentum N=20) remains a research candidate only. Every implementation step requires separate, explicit operator approval before any code is written or run.
